title: Following Up Without Sounding Pushy
description: 今天练习温和跟进：提醒别人、确认进度，但不显得咄咄逼人.

items.cn: 用来发消息或邮件跟进，比直接催促柔和.
examples: Just checking in to see if you had a chance to look at this.
Just checking in. Are we still good for tomorrow?
Just checking in on the timeline for this.
dialogue: Just checking in. Are we still meeting at three?

items.cn: 表示不想施压，但希望对方注意到这件事.
examples: No rush, but could you send it over when you get a chance?
No rush, but I wanted to make sure this didn't get buried.
No rush, but do you have a rough timeline?
dialogue: No rush, but do you have a rough timeline for the draft?
I can get it to you by Thursday.

items.cn: 礼貌地请求别人做一件事，很高频.
examples: When you get a chance, could you review the doc?
Can you send me the link when you get a chance?
When you get a chance, let me know what you think.
dialogue: When you get a chance, could you take a look at the notes?
Sure. I'll check them after lunch.

items.phrase: I just wanted to flag this.
items.cn: 适合指出风险、变化或需要注意的点，不带责备感.
examples: I just wanted to flag this before the meeting.
I just wanted to flag that the numbers may change.
I just wanted to flag a possible issue with the timeline.
dialogue: I just wanted to flag that the client hasn't confirmed the date yet.
Good point. Let's not announce anything until they do.

items.cn: 比 OK 更自然完整，安排时间地点时很好用.
examples: That works for me if it works for you.
